background: Twenty-six cancer deaths were observed in a cohort of 556 insulation workers. Based on national statistics, a cohort of this size and age distribution was expected to experience 14.4 incident cases during the observation period. Therefore, under the null hypothesis, $p_0=14.4 / 556=0.02590$.

task.query: Test whether the observed incidence is significantly greater than expected. Show all hypothesis-testing steps.
task.answer:
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: p=0.0259$ versus $H_2: p \neq 0.0259$ B. Test statistic. First check whether the Normal approximation can be used: $n p_0 q_0=(556)(0.02590)(1-0.0259)=14.0$. Therefore, the Normal ( $z$ statistic) method is OK.

$$
\begin{aligned}
& S E_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\frac{0.02590 \cdot(1-0.02590)}{556}}=0.00674 \
& z_{\text {stat }}=(0.0468-0.0259) /(0.00674)=3.10
\end{aligned}
$$

C. $P$-value. $P=0.0020$
